children.text: Documenting the days of District past
We are currently rebuilding our site. This section inparticular requires some work to add additional content and photos, to existing and new articles.  Please revisit for new content.
If you have any content you would like to share, please send to:
mail@obpsfa.com

mainImage.caption: 2003-2004 squad

seo.description: Photos and stories of the District.

children.text: On Tuesday morning the blue squad knew they had a tough game ahead of them nevertheless they got into the minibus and made their way to the pitches . Once we had kicked off not many chances were ended successfully however challenges were made and we were playing well.At the start of the second-half orpington had a few chances.. played in by some of our central midfielders.The boys played well for a solid 10 minutes after a chance was put away by a Plymouth player the boys heads dropped

Slightly after a few minutes of good play from both teams are Plymouth player scored for them were now giving up in the head. But orpington carried on battling until the end of last minute chance for the blues came to nothing and the game had finished we knew we had let ourselves down
Aqua park

After the game all we wanted to do was to do something fun so we did we walked to Aqua splash when we arrived the boys got changed and ran into the pool with smiles on their facesWe all went off into different some to the flumes and some to the diving board plus many more areas of fun after an hour or so of fun a few of the boys went over to Steve who was half asleep we woke him and told him

That some of the boys wanted to get changed and get out the boys that want you to do so got changed and waited for the others when everybody was out the boys will lead back to the hotel and got changed for a lovely meal of roast duck.
Written by Toby Waller 50%
Jack Lee 50%
Will coats 0% fast asleep

seo.description: On Tuesday morning the blue squad knew they had a tough game ahead of them nevertheless they got into the minibus and made their way to the pitches.

children.text: Today the blue squad played Potters Bar in a match where both sides fought hard to win. Just under a minute in to the game, Mark Cahill placed a wonderful ball to Will Coates who slotted it into the bottom left corner to put the blues 1-0 up. A few minutes later Potters Bar won a corner which was put into the box and tapped in by their defender. The game was now there to be won. We held on till half time. After the half time break, both teams returned on to the pitch and battled out the 2nd half. It was a very tight and well battled second half with both teams having good chances but with 5 minutes left Potters Bar scored from another corner. The blues heads were down and there was only 4 minutes to get an equaliser. Potters Bar thought they had won and late on the blues had a chance with a freekick. The ball was whipped in by Mark Cahill and the defender made a solid clearance but Teddy won the ball back and put a brilliant ball in to Bradley May who headed home an equaliser. The blues now knew they could go on to win the game but the referee blew the final whistle and the game was over. The blues had put in a solid performance and got a good point.

seo.authorDisplay: Bradley May, Mark Cahill, Freddie Fletcher
seo.description: Today the blue squad played Potters Bar in a match where both sides fought hard to win.

children.text: To start the day off, we went to breakfast at 8:30 and we all enjoyed engulfing a lovely full English. After that, we packed our bags ready for a big day because we played Jersey year-fives academy team. We had a bit of a mix around and played in unusual positions. Our keeper,Joseph, who played left midfield scored the winning goal to end the match 2-1 and made our win to loss ratio 2/0 Joe Rowland played in goal and almost kept a clean sheet. Next along the line, we all went to fun exciting Aqua Splash, which is a water park. We played tag in the wave machine and the outside pool. We also went down the flumes. Most of us had pork steak. We hope to win tomorrow.

seo.description: To start the day off, we went to breakfast at 8:30 and we all enjoyed engulfing a lovely full English.

children.text: the moment we are writing this, we are about to go down for dinner. Roast Beef, but we are going to start off by telling you our overall result of our match against the confident Gloucester.
This morning the weather brilliant and it was very hot, as we kicked off. We were the dominate side creating many more chances than the Gloucester. By the half time there were no goals scored.
However we had come closer to goal but we could not finish it into until Alex finished the ball into the back of the net.
During the final few minutes, Gloucester were realy pushing for a goal but we held them off and the final whistle went.

After the match we came back to the hotel for a well deserved fish and chip lunch. In the afternoon we played on the beach at St Brelades bay to relax and have some fun. We wont say what Charlie did !!!!!!!!!

JOSEPH BERMINGHAM 95%
CHARLIE CORDINER 5%
HARRY SCOTT ( supervising role )

seo.description: the moment we are writing this, we are about to go down for dinner. Roast Beef, but we are going to start off by telling you our overall result of our match against the confident Gloucester.

children.text: Today, the Orpington blues battled hard against Erdington and saltley.it was a tough game although we came from one nil down to two one up by the end of the first half. Unfortunately, the lads switched off towards the end and the opposition scored, it was now 2-2. It was a tight game until Mark Cahill broke through scoring a screamer but unfortunately due to the ref’s decision the goal was disallowed from then on the boys head dropped thinking there was no way out of a draw or losing the game. Although there was still hope the boys carried on fighting until the end. During the last quarter of the game, the boys started to almost “give up”, until Ben Duncan had a last-minute run, he went past one and rolled Will in. Will was clearly through, or we at least had a chance of scoring another goal. Sadly, the ref blew the final whistle and the game was over.

seo.authorDisplay: Teddy Gatsell ,Jimi Botton,Mark Cahill, Max Farrell
seo.description: Today, the Orpington blues battled hard against Erdington and saltley.it was a tough game although we came from one nil down to two one up by the end of the first half.
